name: Drunken Rump Roast- Master Recipe (Rr)
recipeIngredient: 3 onions, large, cut into 1/4 inch dice
3 carrots, medium, cut into 1/4 inch dice
recipeInstructions:
1. Tie the peppercorns, cloves, bay leaf, and thyme in a piece of damp cheesecloth.
2. Heat the oil in a large, heavy, nonreactive pot set over high heat. Add the roast and sear on all sides until browned.
3. Add the beer and herb packet to the pot. Bring to a boil, cover tightly, reduce heat, and simmer gently for 1 1/4 hours.
4. Add the leeks and onions, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
5. Add the carrots and salt, and simmer for another 30 minutes.
6. Remove the roast, keep it warm, discard the cheesecloth packet, and strain. Reserve the broth and vegetable mixture separately. There should be about 6 1/2 cups of broth.
7. Skim off the fat and reserve 1/2 cup of broth. Refrigerate the remaining broth for use in Cheddar-Beer Soup (see recipe).
8. Carve four 3/8 inch thick slices of rump roast against the grain. Drizzle with some of the reserved broth. Top each with 2-3 tablespoons of the vegetable mixture. Serve immediately. Wrap and refrigerate the remaining roast for later in the week.
9. Serves 4 with leftovers.
Notes:
Use leftovers in: Cheddar-Beer Soup, Shredded Beef and Vegetable Salad, Baked Beefy Tomato Rigatoni, Open-Faced Rump Roast Sandwiches, or Rump Roast Casserole.
